last_assistant_message: Deployed. Check https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app — the fix loads Chart.js and Leaflet via direct DOM script injection in the correct order (Chart.js first, then Leaflet, then dashboard JS), so the charts and tables should now render.

Deployed. The approach is now completely different — no React hydration of the dashboard at all:

- **`/` redirects to `/dashboard.html`** — the exact same static HTML file that works locally
- **Charts, tables, map** all render via the original inline `<script>` — no Next.js interference
- **News ribbon** — scrolling ticker below the topbar with live headlines from `/api/news` (Google News RSS for "venezuela oil production")
- **Live prices** — fetches `/api/prices` on load and updates the Brent/WTI ticker values

Check https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app now — should show all charts and data since it's the same HTML that works on your local machine.

Fixed. The API returns `{items: [...]}` but the JS was expecting a bare array. Now it reads `data.items`. The news ribbon should scroll with headlines like "US broadly authorizes transactions with Venezuela's oil company PDVSA" from Reuters, AP News, Axios, etc.

Deployed. Here's what changed:

**Mobile UI** — three breakpoints:
- **900px**: sidebar collapses
- **768px**: KPI grid → 2 columns, all chart rows → single column, smaller chart heights, scenario descriptions hidden, tighter table cells
- **480px**: further compressed KPIs, topbar simplified to just brand, modal goes 95vw

**News ribbon** — slowed from 60s to 140s (more than 2x slower, easy to read). Pauses on hover.

**Multi-source news** — now queries 4 Google News RSS feeds in parallel:
1. Venezuela oil production + sanctions
2. PDVSA + Chevron + Venezuela
3. Brent crude + Hormuz
4. OPEC + oil market

Results are deduped, sorted by recency, top 15 articles. Much fresher and broader coverage.

**X posts ready** — `/api/x` endpoint built and wired into the ribbon. When you add a `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` env var in Vercel, it'll fetch recent tweets about Venezuela oil/PDVSA and show them with a blue "X" tag in the ribbon (higher-speed alpha). To set it up:

```
vercel env add TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN
```

Until then, the ribbon runs on news RSS only — no errors, graceful fallback.
Slowed to 300s (5 minutes for a full cycle). Should feel like a slow, readable crawl now.

Done. 

**News ribbon** — now `position:fixed` at the top of the viewport, stays visible as you scroll through the entire dashboard.

**Footer** — updated with categorized sources and update cadence:
- Prices: every 5 min (Yahoo Finance live)
- News: every 30 min (Google News RSS + X API)
- Production: monthly (OPEC/CEIC)
- Dynamic "Last loaded" timestamp that updates on each page visit

Done. The layout is now:

```
VNZLA OIL | Venezuela Crude Oil Production Intelligence | 2026 Guidance Dashboard
LIVE  [====== scrolling news ribbon fills the rest of the bar ======]
BRENT MAY $118.35 +5.0% | BRENT JUN $108.00 ...
```

The nav bar with LIVE + ribbon sits between the topbar and the ticker, stays fixed while you scroll since it's outside the `.main` scroll container.

Deployed. The mobile layout now:

**768px and below** (phones):
- Topbar collapses to just "VNZLA OIL" brand + timestamp
- Ticker becomes horizontally scrollable (swipe)
- KPIs → 2 columns
- All chart rows → single column (stacked)
- Charts shorter (180/160px)
- Scenarios → single column, descriptions hidden
- Tables → horizontal scroll with touch momentum
- Map → 280px height
- Everything gets tighter padding/font sizes
- `overflow-x:hidden` on body prevents any horizontal bleed

**390px** (small phones like iPhone SE):
- Even smaller KPIs and charts
- Scenario cards hidden entirely
- Map → 220px

Much fresher — **13 minutes old** for the newest article, vs 2+ hours before. 20 articles total from Bloomberg, OilPrice, FXEmpire, WION, and Google News aggregation.

The improvement:
- **Direct publisher RSS feeds** (Bloomberg, OilPrice, CNBC) give near-real-time articles
- **Google News `when:1d`** filters to last 24h only
- **10-minute cache** instead of 30 minutes
- **Oil keyword filter** ensures non-oil articles from general feeds get dropped

Deployed. Two fixes:

**News ribbon on mobile** — the `.nav` now has `overflow:hidden` + `position:relative` so the ribbon's absolute-positioned scrolling inner div works within the nav bar height on mobile.

**Chart filters** — the panel header uses `flex-wrap:nowrap` + `overflow-x:auto` instead of `flex-wrap:wrap`. All controls (Monthly/Quarterly/Annual | 1Y/3Y/5Y/Max | Projections) stay in one horizontal row. If the screen is too narrow, you can swipe to see the rest — no awkward second line. Title also shortens to "Prod & Scenarios" on mobile.

Deployed. Two things fixed:

**Mobile news ribbon** — replaced the fixed 300s CSS animation with JS-driven adaptive speed. It now calculates content width vs container width and sets speed at 50px/sec, so on a phone screen (narrow container) the ribbon moves at a readable pace instead of crawling invisibly.

**EN/ES language toggle** — click `EN / ES` in the top-right corner of the topbar. Switches ~60 strings:
- KPI labels (Producción, Deuda Externa, etc.)
- All panel titles (Producción Histórica y Escenarios 2026, etc.)
- Sidebar sections (Cronología de Sanciones, Los Seis Grandes, etc.)
- Scenario cards (Pesimista, Base, Optimista)
- Context headings
- Chart filter labels
- Footer link

Default is English. The data values (numbers, company names, ticker prices) stay as-is since they're universal.

Done.

**Table dividers** — vertical borders between every column (both headers and data cells), last column excluded. Makes it much easier to track rows across columns on mobile when scrolling horizontally.

**i18n removed** — language toggle, all `data-i18n` attributes, translation dictionary, and toggle functions all stripped. Clean slate.

Done. Desktop table looks clean again (no dividers). On mobile, each column now has a fixed pixel width (`table-layout:fixed`) so as you scroll horizontally, the columns stay perfectly aligned — Company 100px, Origin 75px, OFAC 90px, Prod 65px, Guidance 90px, Share 60px, Key JVs 130px. Text that overflows gets ellipsis-truncated.

The fix: set a hard total table width of 610px with `table-layout:fixed` and `max-width:0` on every cell. This forces the browser to respect the exact column widths instead of auto-expanding cells based on content. The share bar is also constrained to 30-40px so it can't push the column wider. All 7 columns should now align perfectly as you scroll.

Dashboard is live at **https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app** with all the GL-52 and NABEP corrections. Here's what updated:

- **NABEP**: excluded → **active at ~55K bpd** under GL-52 (Petrozamora, Junín Sur, Petrocedeño upgrader)
- **GL-52** added to sidebar timeline (Mar 18) + GL-46B/48A/49A (Mar 13)
- **Production baseline**: 903K → 958K (adds NABEP's 55K)
- **KPI**: operators now shows "7+" with GL-52 reference
- **Ticker**: reflects GL-52 opening to all US entities
- **Country bar**: US production 242K → 297K (Chevron + NABEP)
- **JV chart**: Petrozamora now shows NABEP as operator
- **Map markers**: Petrozamora + Petrocedeño updated to GL-52 active
- **Context section**: mentions GL-52 broadening access beyond Big Six

Good call checking the OFAC page directly — GL-52 was a major miss. That March 18 license fundamentally changes the landscape.

Done. The map now has **9 oil spill markers** (red circles with glow border) alongside the infrastructure:

**Spills mapped:**
- **LOL-3 Pipeline, Gulf of Coro** (Jan 4, 2025) — ~90,000 bbl, 70th spill in this area since 2019
- **Amuay Refinery overflow** (Late Jan 2025)
- **Lake Maracaibo chronic leaks** (ongoing) — ~1,000 bbl/day, 13,000 km² estuary
- **LOL Pipeline, Mitare Beach** (Feb 5, 2025) — >150K bbl cumulative, 7,000 fishing families affected
- **Monagas–José Pipeline** (Late Feb 2025) — >24,000 bbl, media blocked by National Guard
- **J-20 Station, Morichal** (~Jan 15, 2025) — up to ~50,000 bbl
- **El Palito / Golfo Triste** (Mar 2025) — 225 km² oil slick, Morrocoy National Park
- **LOL Pipeline, Carirubana** (Mar 25, 2026) — near Cardón refinery
- **LOL Pipeline, Miranda** (Mar 26, 2026) — 2nd failure within 24h

Click any red marker for full details: cause, volume, status, environmental impact, and source. Use the **Spills** filter tab to isolate just the spill markers.

All working:

- **Brent**: $109.05 (live)
- **WTI**: $112.06 (live)  
- **Merey**: $100.05 (derived, $9 spread from Brent)
- **Petcoke**: $1.84 (live from Yahoo Finance — that's per pound, which is correct for fuel-grade petcoke)
- **Production**: Fallback data (need EIA key for live)

**To activate live EIA production data**, register for a free API key at https://www.eia.gov/opendata/ (instant approval), then:

```
! cd projects/vnzla-oil && vercel env add EIA_API_KEY
```

Paste the key when prompted. The `/api/production` endpoint will then pull real monthly data directly from EIA and auto-update the production KPI on the dashboard.

**What's now automated:**

| Data | Source | Update Cadence | Status |
|------|--------|---------------|--------|
| Brent/WTI | Yahoo Finance | Every 5 min | Live |
| Merey | Derived from Brent | Every 5 min | Live |
| Petcoke | Yahoo Finance | Every 5 min | Live |
| News | 7 RSS feeds | Every 10 min | Live |
| Production | EIA API | Monthly (cron 1st) | Needs API key |
| Company data | Hardcoded | Manual via GitHub push | Working |
| OFAC/spills | Hardcoded | Manual via GitHub push | Working |

For the manual data (companies, OFAC, spills) — whenever you hear about changes, just tell me here and I'll update + push. Vercel catches it in ~30 seconds.

**Daily intel agent is set up and first run triggered.**

**`daily-vnzla-intel`** — https://claude.ai/code/scheduled/trig_0162fyfUkbzTsm95DuNT9kku

| | |
|---|---|
| **Schedule** | Every day at 9am ET (1pm UTC) |
| **Model** | Sonnet 4.6 |
| **Repo** | `papiluxo/vnzla-oil` |
| **What it does** | Clones the repo, searches 12+ sources (OFAC, Google News, OPEC, company-specific queries in EN+ES), compares against current dashboard data, makes targeted edits if anything changed, commits + pushes to GitHub → Vercel auto-deploys |

**Daily sweep covers:**
1. OFAC sanctions changes (new GLs, amendments, revocations)
2. Oil spills (EN + Spanish sources)
3. Company news for all 14 operators individually
4. Production data (OPEC, PDVSA, Trading Economics)
5. Merey spread drift

If nothing new is found on a given day, it logs "No updates" and doesn't push. You can check run history at the link above.

**Still need from you:** Register for a free EIA API key at https://www.eia.gov/opendata/ and add it:
```
! cd projects/vnzla-oil && vercel env add EIA_API_KEY
```

That unlocks live monthly production data from the US government instead of our hardcoded fallback.

Fixed. EIA returns values in TBPD (thousand barrels per day), so 905 = 905K bpd. Now displays as `905K bpd` instead of `905000`.

- **Brent**: `$109.05/bbl`
- **WTI**: `$112.06/bbl`
- **Merey**: `$100.05/bbl`
- **Petcoke**: `$1.84/st` (per short ton)

Units persist through live price updates too — the JS uses `innerHTML` with the unit spans instead of `textContent`.

Done. Ticker is now two rows:

**Row 1 — Prices:**
`BRENT MAY $109.05/bbl | BRENT JUN $108.00/bbl | WTI $112.06/bbl | MEREY ~$100/bbl | PETCOKE ~$45/st`

Everything visible now — no cutoff.
Done. Tickers now use `justify-content:space-between` — each tick spreads evenly across the full screen width on desktop, with smart auto-spacing between them. Mobile keeps the scroll behavior.

Done. The ticker is now a single scrolling ribbon — same approach as the news ribbon:

`BRENT MAY $109.05/bbl +5.0% | BRENT JUN $108.00/bbl | WTI $112.06/bbl | MEREY ~$100/bbl | PETCOKE $1.84/st | VNZLA PROD 958–1,021K | OFAC GL-52 OPEN | CHN/RUS EXCLUDED | HORMUZ DISRUPTED | GL-50A BIG SIX | RESERVES 303B bbl`

All in one line, scrolling at 60px/sec, seamless loop, adapts to any viewport width. Same behavior on desktop and mobile. Live prices update both copies in the ribbon.

Back to the static two-row layout:

**Row 1:** `BRENT MAY $109.05/bbl | BRENT JUN $108.00/bbl | WTI $112.06/bbl | MEREY ~$99/bbl | PETCOKE ~$45/st`

**Row 2:** `VNZLA PROD 958–1,021K | GL-50A BIG SIX | GL-52 OPEN | RESERVES 303B bbl`

Done. One line:

`BRENT MAY $109.05/bbl +5.0% | BRENT JUN $108.00/bbl | WTI $112.06/bbl | MEREY ~$99/bbl -$9 spread | PETCOKE ~$45/st`

Fixed. Single line:

`BRENT MAY $109.05/bbl | BRENT JUN $108.00/bbl | WTI $112.06/bbl | MEREY ~$99/bbl | PETCOKE ~$45/st | VNZLA PROD 958–1,021K | RESERVES 303B bbl`

Fixed. The production KPI now dynamically shows:

- **Label**: `Production (Dec 2025)` — actual EIA period, not hardcoded
- **Value**: `905K bpd` — EIA's latest figure
- **Source**: `EIA (live)`
- **Change**: `-9.9% from Nov (1005K)` — real MoM comparison from EIA data

Everything updates automatically as EIA publishes new months. No more mixing of stale manual estimates with live data.

Now the KPI will show:

- **Production (Mar 2026)**: `1090K bpd`
- **Source**: `Kpler/vessel exports`
- **Change**: `+18.1% from Feb (903K)`

The production API now merges two layers:
1. **Recent estimates** (Jan–Mar 2026) from CEIC, Trading Economics, Kpler — updated by the daily intel agent
2. **EIA official data** (through Dec 2025) — auto-fetched via API

Each data point carries its source so you always know where the number came from. As EIA publishes newer months, they automatically replace the estimates.

Done. The historical production chart now updates live from the same `/api/production` data as the KPI. On page load:

1. Fetches `/api/production` (EIA + recent estimates)
2. Updates the **KPI** (Production Mar 2026: 1,090K bpd — Kpler)
3. Injects values into **dataM** (monthly), **dataQ** (quarterly), **dataA** (annual) arrays — overwriting hardcoded values with live data
4. Calls `buildChart()` to redraw the historical chart

So when you switch between Monthly/Quarterly/Annual views, the chart reflects the latest real data — including the Mar 2026 recovery to 1,090K from Kpler vessel tracking. As the daily intel agent or EIA publishes new months, they'll flow through automatically.

Done. The historical chart now has three production lines:

- **Blue solid — EIA Official**: Government data, ~3 month lag (currently through Dec 2025). The authoritative source.
- **Green dashed — Live Estimate**: CEIC/Trading Economics/Kpler data (Jan–Mar 2026). Faster but less rigorous.
- **Amber filled — Blended**: Average of both where they overlap. Where only one source exists for a period, it uses that. This is the "best available" view.

Plus the **red dashed scenario** projection (toggleable).

Where EIA and estimates overlap (e.g. both have a Nov 2025 figure), the blended line shows the average. In the recent months where only estimates exist, blended equals the estimate. In the older months where only EIA exists, blended equals EIA. As EIA publishes new months, the blended line automatically incorporates both.

Fixed. The chart now works in two phases:

**Before API loads** (instant render): All three lines show the hardcoded historical data (2015–2026) so the chart is fully populated immediately — no empty gap.

**After API loads** (~1 sec): 
- **EIA Official** (blue): hardcoded baseline overwritten with live EIA values where they exist (through Dec 2025)
- **Live Estimate** (green dashed): only appears for Jan–Mar 2026 where CEIC/TE/Kpler data exists — so it visually extends beyond where the EIA line ends
- **Blended** (amber filled): average where both sources overlap, else whichever is available. Full continuous line from 2015 to present.

The historical data from 2015–2023 stays solid since that's settled data. The divergence between lines only becomes visible in the recent months where EIA lags and estimates fill the gap.

Done. Default view on page load is now:

- **Monthly** granularity (highlighted)
- **3Y** range (highlighted) — shows ~2023 to present
- **Projections off** — scenario cards collapsed, toggle dot gray, no red dashed line
- Three visible lines: EIA Official (blue), Live Estimate (green dashed), Blended (amber)

Users can still toggle projections on and switch to Annual/Quarterly/1Y/5Y/Max as needed.

Fixed. The Merey KPI card now updates live from the same API as the ticker — price, spread, and YoY change all sync. Both the ticker `MEREY $100.05/bbl` and the KPI card `~$100` will show the same derived price.

Done. Three fixes:

**Chart**: EIA and Live Estimate lines now share the same historical data — they overlap completely through ~2023, then diverge only in recent months where estimates exist separately from EIA. The blended (amber) line only becomes visible where the two sources differ. Much cleaner.

**Merey KPI**: Now matches Brent's format exactly — green color, `$100.05` (no tilde), cents as smaller text, spread shown below.

**VNZLA PROD ticker**: Shows EIA official only — `905K DEC 25 EIA`. Updates live from the API filtering for `source=EIA` specifically, not the estimates.

Fixed both:

**KPI colors** — consistent scheme:
- **Production**: white value, muted unit
- **Brent**: green value
- **Merey**: green value (matching Brent format)
- **Operators**: white value, green subtext
- **Revenue**: green value
- **Debt**: red value, red change
- All subtexts: `t3` (muted gray)

**March in chart** — the trim logic now checks `liveEst` before cutting trailing labels. So when projections are off, `Mar 26` stays visible because the API has Kpler's 1,090K estimate for it. The live estimate (green dashed) line now extends through March showing the divergence from where EIA stops at December.

Done. Here's the new setup:

**Chart header controls:**
`Monthly | Quarterly | Annual | 1Y | 3Y | 5Y | Max | ● Unofficial Est. | ● Projections`

**Default view:** Monthly 3Y with just the **EIA Official** blue line. Clean, single line.

**Toggles:**
- **Unofficial Est.** (green dot) — turns on the green dashed line showing CEIC/Kpler data. Overlaps EIA historically, diverges in recent months where EIA hasn't published yet (Jan-Mar 2026)
- **Projections** (amber dot) — turns on scenario line + bear/base/bull cards

**External debt KPI:** Now shows `-$160B` with the negative sign.

Fixed all four issues:

**EIA line accuracy** — Jan/Feb 2026 no longer show on the EIA Official line. The line now nulls out any period beyond EIA's actual latest (Dec 2025). Those months only appear when you toggle on Unofficial Est.

**No empty space** — chart trims to the last EIA data point by default. When you toggle Unofficial Est. on, it extends to show Mar 2026 (Kpler 1,090K).

**Removed lag text** — legend just says "EIA Official" now, no "(3-mo lag)".

**6M default** — range options are now `6M | 1Y | 3Y | 5Y | Max` with 6M highlighted. Default view shows ~Oct 2025 through Dec 2025, a tight window of the most recent EIA data.

Done. Three fixes:

**Production KPI** — now shows `Production (Mar 2026 est.)` with "est." suffix when the latest data point is from an unofficial source. Sub-text shows the actual source name (e.g. "Kpler/vessel exports"). When EIA catches up and becomes the latest, it drops the "est." suffix.

**Parallel fetches** — all 4 API calls (prices, production, news, X) now fire simultaneously via `Promise.all`. If one API is slow or hangs, the others still complete and update their sections. No more stuck loading.

**Debt KPI** — already has `-$160B` from the previous commit.

Deployed. Changes:

- **8-second timeout** on every API fetch — if any endpoint hangs, it's killed after 8s and returns null. Dashboard continues with whatever data it has.
- **Deleted the two stale analytics branches** (those were auto-created by Vercel when you enabled analytics)
- **Fresh production deploy** on clean main

Try reloading https://vnzla-oil.vercel.app now. If it still doesn't load, can you open browser dev tools (F12 → Console tab) and tell me what error shows? That'll pinpoint the exact issue.
Same bug as before — `let estOn` was declared after the chart code that calls `getDataForView()` which references it. Moved it up with the other state variables. JS validates clean. Should load now.

Both contracts live:

- **Brent front-month (May)**: $109.05 — `BZ=F`
- **Brent next-month (Jul)**: $99.28 — `BZN26.NYM`
- **WTI**: $112.06
- **Merey**: $100.05 (derived)

Both update every 5 minutes. The ticker now shows separate live prices for each Brent contract instead of the same number twice.

**Chart fix**: the 6M range now anchors from the last data point with actual values (Feb 2026 at index ~61) instead of the last label (Dec 2026E at index ~71), so you see Oct 2025 → Feb 2026 instead of an empty chart.

Done. The x-axis now dynamically adjusts based on what's visible:

**EIA only (default):** Chart ends at **Dec 2025** (last EIA data point). No trailing empty space.

**Toggle on Unofficial Est.:** Chart extends to **Mar 2026** (Kpler 1,090K). The green dashed line shows Jan 924K → Feb 903K → Mar 1,090K where EIA has no data yet.

**Toggle on Projections:** Chart extends to **EOY 2026** with scenario line.

The 6M/1Y/3Y/5Y/Max ranges all anchor from the last visible data point too, so switching between them always shows a meaningful window.

Done. Three changes:

**X-axis fix** — `hasVisibleData()` now properly checks what's actually rendered after live data loads. When EIA is the only visible line, chart ends at Dec 2025. When unofficial est is on, extends to Mar 2026. No more empty trailing space.

**Unofficial Est. default ON** — both lines show on initial load. The green dashed line overlaps EIA through historical data and diverges Jan-Mar 2026 where EIA hasn't published yet.

**Pulsing live dot** — a subtle green pulse animates on the last data point of the unofficial estimate line (Mar 2026). Uses CSS `@keyframes livePulse` — gentle opacity/scale oscillation over 2 seconds. Disappears when you toggle the estimate off.

Fixed. Clicking the legend text now calls the same `toggleEst()` / `toggleProj()` functions as the header buttons — so the toggle dot, x-axis trim, scenario card collapse, and dataset visibility all stay in sync regardless of whether you click the legend or the button.

Fixed. The quarterly view now shows the unofficial estimate for Q1 2026. The issue was that `hasVisibleData` was looking for `liveEst['2026-Q1']` (which doesn't exist) instead of checking the monthly keys within the quarter (`2026-01`, `2026-02`, `2026-03`). The new `hasLiveData` helper resolves quarterly/annual keys down to their constituent monthly keys when checking either EIA or estimate data sources.

So on the quarterly view with unofficial est on, Q1 2026 shows the green dashed line with the latest monthly value from that quarter (Mar 2026: 1,090K from Kpler).

Fixed. Quarterly and annual values now average all available months:

- **Q1 2026 unofficial est**: avg(Jan 924 + Feb 903 + Mar 1,090) = **972K** instead of just Mar 1,090K
- Same logic applies to EIA quarterly/annual aggregation

Done. Two changes:

**Larger fonts** — everything bumped 1-2px for readability:
- KPI values: 20px → 24px
- KPI labels: 8px → 10px
- Body text: 12px → 13px
- Tables, sidebar, callouts, ticker, news — all increased
- Mobile sizes untouched

**EN/ES toggle** — click `EN / ES` in the top-right of the header bar:
- 80+ translated strings: KPIs, panel titles, scenario cards, table headers, map filters, sidebar sections, context paragraphs, infrastructure labels, footer
- Uses DOM TreeWalker to find and swap matching text nodes
- Fully reversible — click again to switch back
- Default: English
